mappings.rationaleAI: Structural engineering covering beams, bridges, concrete, and timber structures directly addresses construction techniques (A.6), structural systems and building components (A.7, A.7.b), and civil engineering works including bridges (B.3).

Civil engineering covering bridges, tunnels, buildings, cathedrals, and lighthouses addresses general building construction (A), dams (B.1), bridges (B.3), underground construction (B.4), and lighthouses (B.6).
Architecture spanning from ancient Egypt through modernism covering form, function, and cultural meaning relates to general building construction (A) and building components (A.7).

Modern architectural use of steel, reinforced concrete, air-conditioning, and lighting directly addresses building materials including metals (A.3.g), concrete (A.3.i), steel construction (A.6.d), and glass (A.3.h).
Materials science covering metals, ceramics, and polymers with cement and sand as exemplars directly addresses building materials (A.3) and testing of building materials (A.4).
Urban planning covering metropolitan growth, infrastructure design, and resilience to natural disasters relates to general building construction and preconstruction planning (A).
Landscape architecture encompassing infrastructure design including motorways, dams, and flood defenses connects to harbour and hydraulic works (B.5).
Physics of sound including acoustic environments and engineering approaches to noise reduction directly addresses acoustics and sound-control techniques (A.8).
Geology covering Earth's structure and resource discovery including stone and building materials connects to stone as a building material (A.3.e) and foundations and footings (A.7.a).
Surveying grew out of navigational science, and this volume illuminates A.2 (surveying procedures) and the precision techniques used to lay out building foundations.
British architecture from medieval times to present covering domestic architecture and building practices relates to general building construction (A).
Engineering covering infrastructure including buildings, roads, and railways addresses general building construction (A), construction machinery (A.5), and civil engineering works (B).
